categoryName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: These pathognomonic hexagonal crystals seen on urinalysis indicate which condition?

explanation.summary: Flat, colorless hexagonal crystals are the classic microscopic finding of cystinuria, reflecting defective reabsorption of cystine and dibasic amino acids.
explanation.whyCorrect: Flat, colorless hexagonal crystals are the classic microscopic finding of cystinuria, reflecting defective reabsorption of cystine and dibasic amino acids.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: This patient shows bluish-black pigment deposition in the sclera and periocular tissue. In a patient with a tyrosine-catabolism enzyme deficiency and urine that darkens on standing, this finding is termed what?

explanation.summary: Ochronosis is deposition of polymerized homogentisic acid pigment in connective tissue and sclera in alkaptonuria (homogentisate oxidase deficiency).
explanation.whyCorrect: Ochronosis is deposition of polymerized homogentisic acid pigment in connective tissue and sclera in alkaptonuria (homogentisate oxidase deficiency).

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: This pathway shows tyrosine degradation with a block at homogentisate oxidase, causing homogentisate to accumulate and form a dark pigment. Which disorder does this represent?

explanation.summary: Deficiency of homogentisate oxidase causes alkaptonuria: homogentisic acid accumulates, darkening urine on standing and depositing in connective tissue (ochronosis).
explanation.whyCorrect: Deficiency of homogentisate oxidase causes alkaptonuria: homogentisic acid accumulates, darkening urine on standing and depositing in connective tissue (ochronosis).

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: This pathway highlights cystathionine beta-synthase, a vitamin B6-dependent enzyme converting homocysteine to cystathionine. Deficiency of this enzyme causes homocystinuria; which classic clinical finding results?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Downward and inward lens dislocation (ectopia lentis)

explanation.summary: Cystathionine beta-synthase deficiency raises homocysteine, damaging connective tissue and causing downward-and-inward ectopia lentis, a marfanoid habitus, thromboembolism, and intellectual disability.
explanation.whyCorrect: Cystathionine beta-synthase deficiency raises homocysteine, damaging connective tissue and causing downward-and-inward ectopia lentis, a marfanoid habitus, thromboembolism, and intellectual disability.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: This figure maps branched-chain amino acid catabolism and its enzyme defects. Propionyl-CoA derived from valine and isoleucine is carboxylated and then converted to succinyl-CoA by a mutase that requires which vitamin cofactor?

explanation.summary: Methylmalonyl-CoA mutase converts L-methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA and is vitamin B12-dependent; its defect produces methylmalonic acidemia, shown at the bottom of the figure.
explanation.whyCorrect: Methylmalonyl-CoA mutase converts L-methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA and is vitamin B12-dependent; its defect produces methylmalonic acidemia, shown at the bottom of the figure.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: This pathway converts propionyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA. A defect in methylmalonyl-CoA mutase (or its cofactor) causes accumulation and urinary excretion of which compound?

explanation.summary: A blocked mutase leaves methylmalonyl-CoA to accumulate and be hydrolyzed to methylmalonic acid, causing methylmalonic acidemia and aciduria.
explanation.whyCorrect: A blocked mutase leaves methylmalonyl-CoA to accumulate and be hydrolyzed to methylmalonic acid, causing methylmalonic acidemia and aciduria.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: This figure shows amino acids and odd-chain fatty acids entering the TCA cycle via methylmalonyl-CoA. An infant deficient in the B12-dependent mutase of this pathway would most likely present acutely with which derangement?

explanation.summary: Mutase or B12 deficiency causes methylmalonic acidemia; accumulated organic acid produces an anion-gap metabolic acidosis, frequently with ketosis and hyperammonemia in infancy.
explanation.whyCorrect: Mutase or B12 deficiency causes methylmalonic acidemia; accumulated organic acid produces an anion-gap metabolic acidosis, frequently with ketosis and hyperammonemia in infancy.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: The highlighted brush-border/kidney transporter for basic (dibasic) amino acids is defective in one inherited disorder. What is the classic clinical consequence of losing this transport system?

explanation.summary: Loss of the B0,+ system for basic amino acids (and cystine) in the intestine and proximal tubule causes cystinuria; poorly soluble cystine precipitates into recurrent hexagonal urinary calculi.
explanation.whyCorrect: Loss of the B0,+ system for basic amino acids (and cystine) in the intestine and proximal tubule causes cystinuria; poorly soluble cystine precipitates into recurrent hexagonal urinary calculi.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: In the pathway branching from tyrosine, the step forming dopamine from dopa (labeled 'requires: PLP') would be impaired by a deficiency of which vitamin?

explanation.summary: PLP (pyridoxal phosphate), the active form of vitamin B6, is the cofactor for the decarboxylation of dopa to dopamine in catecholamine synthesis.
explanation.whyCorrect: PLP (pyridoxal phosphate), the active form of vitamin B6, is the cofactor for the decarboxylation of dopa to dopamine in catecholamine synthesis.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: The cycle shown regenerates the tetrahydrobiopterin (BH4) needed by phenylalanine hydroxylase. A deficiency of dihydropteridine reductase would most directly cause which laboratory picture?

explanation.summary: BH4 is the essential cofactor for phenylalanine hydroxylase; failure to regenerate it (dihydropteridine reductase deficiency) produces a 'malignant' PKU-type hyperphenylalaninemia even when the hydroxylase enzyme is normal.
explanation.whyCorrect: BH4 is the essential cofactor for phenylalanine hydroxylase; failure to regenerate it (dihydropteridine reductase deficiency) produces a 'malignant' PKU-type hyperphenylalaninemia even when the hydroxylase enzyme is normal.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: This biosynthetic pathway converts tryptophan into a signaling molecule. The final product is a neurotransmitter regulating mood, sleep, and appetite. What is it?

explanation.summary: Tryptophan is hydroxylated (tryptophan hydroxylase, BH4) to 5-hydroxytryptophan, then decarboxylated (PLP-dependent) to serotonin, a monoamine neurotransmitter.
explanation.whyCorrect: Tryptophan is hydroxylated (tryptophan hydroxylase, BH4) to 5-hydroxytryptophan, then decarboxylated (PLP-dependent) to serotonin, a monoamine neurotransmitter.

questions.topicName: Amino Acid Metabolism II: Inborn Errors
questions.stem: The copper-dependent enzyme that converts tyrosine toward melanin in melanocytes is highlighted. A hereditary deficiency of this enzyme causes which condition?

explanation.summary: Tyrosinase (Cu2+-dependent) catalyzes the first step of melanin synthesis; its loss causes oculocutaneous albinism with absent pigment in skin, hair, and eyes.
explanation.whyCorrect: Tyrosinase (Cu2+-dependent) catalyzes the first step of melanin synthesis; its loss causes oculocutaneous albinism with absent pigment in skin, hair, and eyes.
